question.text: If both options in a bull call spread are exercised (USDINR ≥ higher strike), the trader:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When USDINR ≥ higher strike: long call payoff = (USDINR - lower strike), short call liability = (USDINR - higher strike). Net = higher strike - lower strike - net premium = max profit. **The Trap:** Above the higher strike, additional gains on the long call are exactly offset by losses on the short call — profit is capped. **Cross-Exam Context:** Spread mechanics are tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This cap on profit is the price paid for reducing the cost of the position (net premium is less than naked call premium).
